title: cornus stolonifera var. elata
intro: Cornus stolonifera var. elata, also known as Red-osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its red stems and clusters of small white flowers. The medicinal properties of Cornus stolonifera var. elata include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as arthritis and gout. The health benefits of consuming this plant are said to include lowering cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Cornus stolonifera var. elata is often combined with other plants to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat various health issues.
